Package org.apache.juneau.marshall.bson

Class BsonParser

java.lang.Object
org.apache.juneau.marshall.Context
org.apache.juneau.marshall.MarshallingContextable
org.apache.juneau.marshall.parser.Parser
org.apache.juneau.marshall.parser.InputStreamParser
org.apache.juneau.marshall.bson.BsonParser
All Implemented Interfaces:
BsonMetaProvider, ArrayRecordReadable, RecordReadable
Direct Known Subclasses:
BsonParser.Base64, BsonParser.SpacedHex
public class BsonParser extends InputStreamParser implements BsonMetaProvider, RecordReadable, ArrayRecordReadable
Parses BSON (Binary JSON) into POJO models.
Media types:
Handles Content-Type types: application/bson
Description

BSON-to-Java type mapping:

  • BSON document (0x03) → bean or Map
  • BSON array (0x04) → Collection or Java array (numeric string keys ignored)
  • BSON string (0x02) → String; coerced to types parseable from string
  • BSON boolean (0x08) → Boolean
  • BSON int32 (0x10) → int; coerced to long, short, byte as needed
  • BSON int64 (0x12) → long; coerced to int if target is int
  • BSON double (0x01) → double; coerced to float
  • BSON datetime (0x09) → Date, Calendar, Instant, or long (millis)
  • BSON binary (0x05) → byte[]
  • BSON decimal128 (0x13) → BigDecimal
  • BSON null (0x0A) → null
  • BSON ObjectId (0x07) → 24-character hex String
  • Deprecated/unsupported types → silently skipped
Notes:
  • This class is thread safe and reusable.
Limitations:
  • BSON requires a top-level document. Standalone scalars are wrapped as {value: x}.
  • Map keys must be strings. Non-string keys use toString(). Null keys use a configurable placeholder.
  • ObjectId (0x07) is read-only; parsed as 24-character hex strings.
  • Deprecated types (Undefined, DBPointer, Symbol, JS with Scope) are silently skipped.
  • MongoDB-internal types (JavaScript, Timestamp, MinKey, MaxKey) are not supported.
  • Decimal128 (0x13) NaN and ±Infinity have no BigDecimal form; they are normalized to null.
  • Wire-declared lengths (strings, binary, document/array size prefixes) are bounds-checked against a configurable maximum (default 16 MiB, see BsonParser.Builder.maxLength(int)); malformed/adversarial lengths fail with a clean ParseException instead of an unchecked error.
  • BSON is binary; SpacedHex and Base64 subclasses accept text-encoded input.
See Also:
  • BSON Basics

    • copy

      public BsonParser.Builder copy()
      Description copied from class: Context
      Creates a builder from this context object.

      Builders are used to define new contexts (e.g. serializers, parsers) based on existing configurations.

      Overrides:
      copy in class Parser
      Returns:
      A new Builder object.

    • createSession

      public BsonParserSession.Builder createSession()
      Description copied from class: Context
      Create a session builder based on the properties defined on this context.

      Use this method for creating sessions where you want to override basic settings. Otherwise, use Context.getSession() directly.

      Overrides:
      createSession in class InputStreamParser
      Returns:
      A new session builder.

    • parseRecords

      public RecordReader parseRecords(Object input) throws IOException
      Convenience delegator for the whole-value RecordReader using default session arguments. The real implementation lives on BsonParserSession.parseRecords(Object).
      Specified by:
      parseRecords in interface RecordReadable
      Parameters:
      input - The input.
      Returns:
      A new RecordReader cursor.
      Throws:
      IOException - If a problem occurred opening the underlying input.

    • parseArrayRecords

      public RecordReader parseArrayRecords(Object input) throws IOException
      Convenience delegator for the buffered array-element RecordReader using default session arguments. The real implementation lives on BsonParserSession.parseArrayRecords(Object).
      Specified by:
      parseArrayRecords in interface ArrayRecordReadable
      Parameters:
      input - The input.
      Returns:
      A buffered RecordReader.
      Throws:
      IOException - If a problem occurred reading the input.

    • isRecordStreaming

      public boolean isRecordStreaming()
      The BSON record cursor is buffered/RecordAdapter-backed, not O(1) streaming.
      Specified by:
      isRecordStreaming in interface RecordReadable
      Returns:
      Always false.
